mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% across 100+ observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series trend upward, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which expands corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns during early-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO uptrends produce negative or flat 20-day equity returns during early-cycle phases, or the mechanism's predictive accuracy drops below baseline (Rank IC < 0.00) in this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains consumer spending capacity and corporate hiring cycles, producing reliably positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of monthly observations, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 320.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches 320.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized positive momentum in quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures confirms robust domestic demand expansion, directly increasing corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day returns, or positive joint momentum yields negative equity returns in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.5 percent signals supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines of this magnitude are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.02 during contraction regimes.
